LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF NATIVIDAD RAMIREZ'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF THE COURTS ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANTS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES IS DENIED. ALTHOUGH MS. RAMIREZ HAS NOT SATISFIED THE REQUIREMENTS FOR RECONSIDERATION PER CCP 1008, THE COURT HAS ON ITS OWN MOTION PER LE FRANCOIS V. GOEL (2005) 35 CAL. 4TH 1094 DONE ANOTHER REVIEW OF THE ORDER GRANTING THE CITY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IN LIGHT OF THE ARGUMENTS AND EVIDENCE PRESENTED IN THIS MOTION AND, BASED ON THAT REVIEW, HAS CONCLUDED THAT THE PRIOR ORDER IS CORRECT AND SHOULD NOT BE DISTURBED IN ANY WAY. THE DEPOSITION OF MR. MA IS NEITHER A "NEW FACT" NOR A BASIS TO CHANGE THE PRIOR ORDER. MS. RAMIREZ'S OPPOSITION TO THE SUMMARY JUDGMENT EXPRESSLY REFERRED TO MR. MA ACTING AS MS. RAMIREZ'S REPLACEMENT. (OPPOSITION MEMO TO SUMMARY JUDGMENT MOTION 7:26-27.). THE COURT PREVIOUSLY DETERMINED THAT THE AGES OF THE PERSONS WHO ASSUMED THE DUTIES THAT MS. RAMIREZ PREVIOUSLY DID WHILE SHE WAS EMPLOYED BY THE DISTRICT ATTORNEY'S OFFICE WERE NOT GROUNDS TO DENY THE CITY'S MOTION. SIMILARLY, THE DEPOSITION OF DISTRICT ATTORNEY GASCON AND THE ATTORNEY GENERAL'S RESPONSE TO MS. RAMIREZ'S SUBPOENA IS NEITHER A "NEW FACT" NOR A BASIS TO CHANGE THE PRIOR ORDER. A FACT OR CIRCUMSTANCE "WHOLLY COLLATERAL TO THE MERITS OF THE INITIAL MOTION" IS INSUFFICIENT TO WARRANT RECONSIDERATION. (GILBERD V. AC TRANSIT, 32 CAL.APP.4TH 1494, 1500; GARCIA V. HEJMADI (1997) 58 CAL.APP.4TH 674, 690.) WHETHER THE DISTRICT ATTORNEY'S OFFICE ACTUALLY CONTACTED THE ATTORNEY GENERAL'S OFFICE HAS NO RELEVANCE TO THE AGE DISCRIMINATION CLAIM. MOST SIGNIFICANTLY, THE COURT REMAINS OF THE VIEW THAT HICKS V. KNTV TELEVISION, INC. (2008) 160 CAL.APP.4TH 994 REQUIRES THAT THE CITY'S MOTION BE GRANTED. PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE FORM OF ORDER THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR NO. 8189

Notice Of Motion For Reconsideration Of The Courts Granting Defendants Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues
Off Calendar Mar-17-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF NATIVIDAD RAMIREZ's Motion For Reconsideration Of The Courts Order Granting Defendants Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues is denied. Although Ms. Ramirez has not satisfied the requirements for reconsideration per CCP 1008, the court has on its own motion per Le Francois v. Goel (2005) 35 Cal. 4th 1094 done another review of the order granting the City's motion for summary judgment in light of the arguments and evidence presented in this motion and, based on that review, has concluded that the prior order is correct and should not be disturbed in any way. The deposition of Mr. Ma is neither a "new fact" nor a basis to change the prior order. Ms. Ramirez's opposition to the summary judgment expressly referred to Mr. Ma acting as Ms. Ramirez's replacement. (Opposition Memo to Summary Judgment Motion 7:26-27.). The court previously determined that the ages of the persons who assumed the duties that Ms. Ramirez previously did while she was employed by the District Attorney's office were not grounds to deny the City's motion. Similarly, the deposition of District Attorney Gascon and the Attorney General's response to Ms. Ramirez's subpoena is neither a "new fact" nor a basis to change the prior order. A fact or circumstance "wholly collateral to the merits of the initial motion" is insufficient to warrant reconsideration. (Gilberd v. AC Transit, 32 Cal.App.4th 1494, 1500; Garcia v. Hejmadi (1997) 58 Cal.App.4th 674, 690.) Whether the District Attorney's office actually contacted the Attorney General's office has no relevance to the age discrimination claim. Most significantly, the court remains of the view that Hicks v. KNTV Television, Inc. (2008) 160 Cal.App.4th 994 requires that the City's motion be granted. Prevailing party shall prepare form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.
